spec_match.rationale: The step-by-step conceptual explanation of native streaming placeholders and ranges is excellent. However, the capability probe checks on line 218 are copy-pasted boilerplates (gpu, mediaDevices, etc.) and completely fail to verify support for HTML ProcessingInstruction nodes or out-of-order streaming.

interactive.rationale: The interactive capability probe buttons work, but their output is useless/irrelevant because it checks unrelated APIs instead of HTML parsing support.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent streaming use case concept description perfectly.

edge_cases.rationale: Provides a solid overview of start/end target reserving compared to standard DOM PIs.

references.rationale: Correctly includes the official ChromeStatus link in the references section.

design_system.rationale: Layout fits the design system with clean numbers, highlight tags, and grid boxes.

openQuestions.title: Fix capability probe to detect native ProcessingInstruction support
openQuestions.detail: Replace the boilerplated checks array on line 217 with a real capability check: parse a test string containing a PI (e.g. '<?test data?>') using DOMParser, and check if a node of type 7 (PROCESSING_INSTRUCTION_NODE) exists in the parsed document.
